description_long: This object is a tall, slender obelisk with a square base and a long, tapering shaft that rises vertically. The base is broad and flat, featuring four outward-angled corner supports that give it a stable, grounded appearance. The shaft is smooth and unadorned, extending upward from the center of the base without any visible segmentation or decorative elements. The entire structure appears to be made from a uniform, light-colored material, likely concrete or stone, with a matte finish. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or ornamentation on any part of the obelisk. The design is minimalist and functional, suitable for placement in urban plazas, parks, or as a commemorative monument.
description: A tall, minimalist obelisk with a square base and four angled corner supports, made from a light, matte material. The shaft is smooth and unadorned, giving it a modern, functional appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the square base with four prominent corner supports and the central shaft rising vertically.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and the uniformity of the base and shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same visible base structure and shaft alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the symmetrical base and shaft, with no additional features visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is square and flat, with four outward-angled supports at each corner, providing stability and a subtle decorative touch. The base appears solid and is constructed from the same material as the shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a simple, pointed extension of the shaft, with no additional decoration or unique features, maintaining the minimalist aesthetic.
